purpose: Machine-readable contract for authentication, trial start, purchase recovery, sync scope, canonical account reads, idempotent learning events, server scheduling, default sync strategy, and offline behavior.

authentication.remote_auth_request_rule: request-code, verify-code, refresh, and logout each use one bounded deadline that includes response validation and parsing; timeout and cancellation are transport outcomes rather than authorization rejection
authentication.authenticated_request_rule: access-token acquisition, the first protected fetch, at most one forced refresh, and one retry share one bounded deadline; caller cancellation or a changed originating session cancels the request, and cancellation itself cannot trigger refresh, invalidate, or mutate a replacement session

default_sync_strategy.learning_state_rule: latest_valid_learning_event_wins_per_card; learning and review completion aggregates plus per-card learning state are derived from accepted events while physical-space and check-in state keep separate authority
default_sync_strategy.physical_space_rule: latest_valid_explicit_space_action_wins unless it violates box knowledge ownership or contract constraints; invalid actions are rejected and client refreshes from server canonical state
default_sync_strategy.physical_space_snapshot_reconciliation: clients preserve each explicit action as a durable credential-free command, read account-level server canonical state before replay, overlay only matching queued actions, and accept the canonical merged response; clients never restore or upload a whole physical-space snapshot
default_sync_strategy.physical_space_write_atomicity: CloudBase account-level canonical reads, legacy migration, dimension-specific merge, immutable action-ledger insert, and state write-back run inside one database transaction with conflict retry
default_sync_strategy.check_in_rule: an authenticated explicit v2 check-in command can only move the account-and-day state monotonically to checked in; learning counters remain event-derived and physical-space counters remain space-derived

canonical_read.identity_rule: derive the account only from the active v2 session; bootstrap never accepts phone_number in its query or body
canonical_read.missing_state_rule: return explicit empty canonical state for a valid account/day/track; never copy device-local state into a canonical read
canonical_read.cursor_rule: return null when the service has no stored scheduler cursor; do not infer exact cross-device position from device-local state or claim exact resume
canonical_read.consistency_rule: generated_at binds one response and each mutable component exposes acknowledged_at freshness; the current CloudBase multi-collection adapter does not claim a serializable cross-collection transaction
canonical_read.content_rule: content version is the SHA-256 of the normalized ordered source; production requires a matching published content-release.v1 descriptor while development may return release_id null
canonical_read.release_publication_rule: the development importer rejects non-null release descriptors; a separate publication pipeline must verify formal content approval before writing content-release.v1
canonical_read.client_rule: an authenticated client may use validated cached state while offline, but must reconcile with this server read before pushing restored state; without previously validated canonical state and required content, mobile keeps a valid session retryable but blocks learning, local persistence of the reconciled snapshot, mutation replay, and product-state writes until bootstrap succeeds and the loaded card-source content version matches
canonical_read.known_gap: the repository-local CloudBase learning-events.v2 backend, explicit daily check-in, physical-space actions, React Native durable producer/replay, server scheduler, and mobile scheduler-session binding are implemented but not deployed; production publication remains unimplemented

endpoint.identity_rule: derive the account only from the active v2 session; reject phone_number, counters, snapshots, and every request field except day_key

command_contract.day_key_rule: require YYYY-MM-DD; the client cannot submit checked_in_today because the endpoint itself is the affirmative action
command_contract.schema_rule: reject unknown fields and any empty, scalar, array, or missing body
command_contract.monotonic_rule: the first valid command moves the account-and-day state to checked in; exact repeats return the already-canonical acknowledgement without another state transition
command_contract.counter_rule: never accept learning, review, favorite, sleeping, pending-review, or total counters; learning aggregates are derived from accepted learning-events.v2 and space aggregates are derived from canonical physical space
command_contract.storage_rule: persist only schema_version, account_key, day_key, checked_in_today true, and acknowledged_at in the independent softbook_daily_check_ins collection
command_contract.storage_adapter_rule: CloudBase may return its system _id; strip only that adapter-owned field before validating the exact five-field business record, and fail closed on every other unknown or malformed field
command_contract.migration_rule: write an independent account-and-day daily-check-in.v2 record and never mutate retained legacy daily documents; first-event migration can still read their unchanged baseline counters while canonical reads overlay the independent check-in
command_contract.transaction_rule: check-in commits monotonically in its own account-and-day record through the same serialized memory store or CloudBase transaction facility; learning-event transactions never overwrite that record, so a concurrent first event cannot lose the action
command_contract.response_rule: return schema_version, day_key, checked_in_today true, and the canonical acknowledged_at; response identity must match the request day

mobile_contract.remote_rule: remote mobile calls only /v2/progress/check-in and sends day_key without phone_number or counters
mobile_contract.trigger_rule: only an explicit user check-in creates the command; learning completion never uploads a daily snapshot because learning-events.v2 owns progress
mobile_contract.offline_rule: persist a credential-free account-scoped check_in_daily_progress command containing only dayKey; replay injects the current access token in memory
mobile_contract.legacy_queue_rule: during hydration convert a persisted sync_daily_progress entry only when its complete legacy snapshot records checkedInToday true, uses a valid day, contains nonnegative integer counters, and has a consistent total; retain only account context and dayKey and discard every other legacy daily snapshot entry
mobile_contract.ack_rule: remove the queued command only after a strict matching daily-check-in.v2 response; a failed, ambiguous, cancelled, or stale-session response keeps the command queued
mobile_contract.recovery_rule: on restart, preserve a queued local check-in only when an exact persisted command matches the active account and bootstrap day; without that command, canonical checked_in_today false clears stale local state, and event-derived counts never confirm check-in
mobile_contract.reconciliation_rule: after remote acknowledgement refresh canonical bootstrap before treating server progress as reconciled

legacy_cutover.scope_rule: no runtime option, legacy token, active v2 session, or unmigrated account can re-enable either snapshot-write route; retained legacy documents are read-only migration input
legacy_cutover.remaining_v1_rule: card-source and membership v1 routes remain a development migration bridge only and production still rejects every v1 route

endpoint.identity_rule: derive the account only from the active v2 session; reject phone_number, day_key, snapshot counters, complete space snapshots, and every unknown request field

command_contract.batch_rule: require one to twenty immutable actions; each contains exactly action_id, card_id, dimension, value, and client_occurred_at
command_contract.action_id_rule: require a 1-128 character opaque identifier; the same account and action_id with an identical canonical payload returns duplicate while any payload mismatch rejects the complete batch with 409
command_contract.dimension_rule: dimension is exactly favorite or sleep and value is boolean; favorite is a tag while sleep excludes the card from learning without deleting scheduler state
command_contract.content_rule: track, content_version, and every card_id must match the current normalized server card source; production additionally requires its published content-release.v1 descriptor
command_contract.time_rule: client_occurred_at is retained for long-offline ordering but rejected when it is more than five minutes ahead of server time
command_contract.merge_rule: favorite and sleep use independent last-writer-wins clocks with action_id as the deterministic equal-time tie breaker, so an action in one dimension cannot overwrite the other
command_contract.transaction_rule: legacy migration, ledger conflict detection, dimension merge, immutable action records, and canonical account state commit atomically; a rejected batch commits nothing
command_contract.response_rule: return one ordered result for every submitted action plus the requested track's canonical projection and matching content_version

storage_contract.state_integrity: stored space-state.v2 business fields and each dimension clock/action pair are validated exactly on every canonical read and write; CloudBase system _id is the only removable adapter field
storage_contract.action_integrity: stored ledger payload, canonical digest, result, acknowledgement, and account ownership are validated exactly before duplicate acknowledgement
storage_contract.legacy_migration: old phone and phone-day space documents are read-only input; the first account-scoped v2 read or write migrates each legacy card into both dimension clocks using last_modified_at and deterministic synthetic action ids without deleting the source documents

mobile_contract.durability_rule: persist an account-scoped credential-free apply_space_action command before optimistic UI authority advances
mobile_contract.request_rule: replay preserves every immutable action field and action_id, injects the current access token in memory, and for the same track binds the request envelope to the currently validated content version; it never rebinds an action across tracks
mobile_contract.ack_rule: remove queued actions only after a strict matching space-actions-ack.v2 result of applied, stale, or duplicate and a canonical projection matching track/content
mobile_contract.legacy_queue_rule: during hydration migrate each valid legacy sync_space_state card snapshot into deterministic favorite and sleep actions, discard phone_number, day_key, counters, tokens, and malformed snapshots, and never replay the original whole snapshot
mobile_contract.recovery_rule: canonical bootstrap is the base; overlay only same-account, same-track durable pending actions in queue order, including actions awaiting same-track content-version rebinding, so unqueued device state can never overwrite server authority
mobile_contract.reconciliation_rule: after remote acknowledgement refresh canonical bootstrap before treating space state and scheduler eligibility as reconciled

legacy_cutover.scope_rule: no runtime option, legacy token, active v2 session, or unmigrated account can re-enable physical-space snapshot reads or writes

endpoint.identity_rule: derive the account only from the active v2 session; reject phone_number or any client-supplied account identity

product_alignment.aggregate_rule: learning and review completion counts plus per-card learning state are derived from accepted immutable events; clients never submit canonical counters or learning snapshots

request_contract.batch_rule: one track per request; require one or more events up to a server-configured bound; preserve input order in the acknowledgement
request_contract.schema_rule: reject unknown fields at the request, event, and device_cursor levels

event_contract.selection_id_rule: copy the opaque selection_id from the current learning-session.v1 selection into the immutable event; for each unseen event the server transaction requires the current account-and-track cursor to match selection_id, card_id, phase, and content_version exactly, while an already accepted exact replay remains valid after that cursor is cleared
event_contract.event_id_rule: generate one opaque stable event_id before durable local enqueue and reuse it unchanged for every retry
event_contract.content_version_rule: bind every event to the exact SHA-256 content version used for the answer; the server validates the track, card, interaction, and retained published release in production
event_contract.client_time_rule: preserve a valid RFC3339 client_occurred_at for bounded activity-day attribution, but never use the client clock as membership, entitlement, idempotency, or canonical ordering authority

device_cursor.device_id_rule: use a pseudonymous installation identifier that contains no account or credential material
device_cursor.sequence_rule: use a positive monotonically increasing safe integer allocated before durable enqueue; gaps and out-of-order replay are valid
device_cursor.authority_rule: device_cursor proves replay provenance and detects forks; it is not the scheduler cursor and does not promise exact cross-device resume

idempotency_and_atomicity.exact_replay: return duplicate with the original server_sequence and do not mutate projections; an already accepted byte-equivalent canonical event remains a duplicate when mutable time or content-retention windows later change
idempotency_and_atomicity.stored_event_integrity: before returning duplicate, recompute the canonical digest from the stored immutable payload and track; any mismatch fails closed without acknowledgement or writes
idempotency_and_atomicity.event_id_conflict: the same event key with a different canonical payload rejects the entire request with HTTP 409
idempotency_and_atomicity.device_cursor_conflict: the same device cursor bound to a different event_id rejects the entire request with HTTP 409
idempotency_and_atomicity.mixed_batch_rule: exact duplicates and at most one unseen event may commit together and each receives an explicit result; the unseen event must consume the current selection and a request with multiple unseen events is rejected atomically
idempotency_and_atomicity.selection_conflict_rule: a missing, stale, cross-account, cross-track, or mismatched selection binding rejects the entire request with HTTP 409 before writes
idempotency_and_atomicity.validation_atomicity: any malformed, unauthorized, unknown-content, or conflicting event rejects the entire request before writes
idempotency_and_atomicity.transaction_atomicity: current selection validation, one new immutable event, account server sequence, derived learning and FSRS projections, all migrated-track learning-session projection watermarks, exact input-track selection clearing, and daily progress commit in one transaction; failure leaves no partial acceptance
idempotency_and_atomicity.cloudbase_atomic_batch_rule: the repository-local CloudBase adapter accepts at most 9 input events but at most one may be unseen; the maximum successful fixture of 8 distinct exact duplicates plus one current-selection event uses 29 operations, and the first-event all-track migration fixture uses no more than 29, below the platform limit of 100 operations per transaction
idempotency_and_atomicity.cloudbase_transaction_query_rule: CloudBase transactions use deterministic document reads and writes only; bounded legacy learning queries run before the transaction and a transactionally written account revision fence forces a retry when a v1 write changes that snapshot

server_authority.server_sequence_rule: assign one stable monotonically increasing account-scoped server_sequence to each newly accepted event
server_authority.duplicate_sequence_rule: an exact duplicate returns the previously assigned server_sequence
server_authority.canonical_ordering_rule: server_sequence is canonical ordering; client time and device sequence never override it
server_authority.per_card_state_rule: the highest valid server_sequence for a card is the canonical latest learning event
server_authority.activity_day_rule: derive the China product activity day from validated client_occurred_at within configured retention and future-skew bounds; otherwise reject the event rather than trust an arbitrary day_key
server_authority.entitlement_rule: event acceptance never grants membership or content access; access remains server-authoritative and content delivery remains independently gated

acknowledgement_contract.retry_rule: a missing or ambiguous response must be retried with byte-equivalent canonical event payloads and the same event IDs
acknowledgement_contract.queue_removal_rule: remove an event from the local queue only after an accepted or duplicate result for that event_id
acknowledgement_contract.canonical_refresh_rule: after replay, read /v2/bootstrap again before presenting reconciled server state or sending dependent mutations

mobile_client_contract.storage_rule: persist the outbox under an independent versioned AsyncStorage key; persist the immutable selection-bound event and allocated installation cursor before advancing the card UI
mobile_client_contract.identity_rule: store the account owner only for local queue isolation; never include phone_number or credential material in the learning-events.v2 request body or event payload
mobile_client_contract.cursor_rule: reuse one pseudonymous installation ID and monotonically allocate a positive safe-integer sequence; repair a stale persisted next sequence without reusing an existing cursor
mobile_client_contract.batch_rule: submit exact duplicates plus at most one unseen selection-bound event for one account and one track without compacting, rewriting, or reordering immutable payloads; end a batch at the first track boundary and never answer a second card before the first acknowledgement, bootstrap reconciliation, and next session read
mobile_client_contract.ack_rule: remove events only after a strict ordered accepted-or-duplicate acknowledgement with matching event IDs and positive unique server sequences
mobile_client_contract.retry_rule: retain byte-equivalent events after ambiguous or failed requests; pause automatic retry after a transient failure until network recovery, app foreground, or a newly durably enqueued event
mobile_client_contract.concurrency_rule: serialize replay per originating session; if a durable event arrives or a dependent mutation finishes queueing after an in-flight pass has read its queue, schedule one follow-up pass instead of running concurrently or waiting for an unrelated trigger
mobile_client_contract.recovery_rule: hydrate the account outbox count with authenticated bootstrap; any pending selection-bound event blocks another card completion until strict acknowledgement, post-acknowledgement bootstrap mapping, and a fresh learning-session read; a cached selected card may be answered offline once, but the client cannot choose or enqueue a second card offline
mobile_client_contract.reconciliation_rule: replay only after validated bootstrap and content hydration; while events are pending, queue dependent check-in and space-state mutations and suppress routine canonical refreshes that would overwrite local intent; after any acknowledgement, keep dependent mutations blocked until another bootstrap is fetched and mapped
mobile_client_contract.logout_rule: clear only the signed-out account's queued events while preserving the installation identity and next sequence
mobile_client_contract.account_switch_rule: scope replay, bootstrap, and authenticated HTTP authorization handling to the originating session identity rather than phone number alone; a stale response from a signed-out or replaced session must not refresh, invalidate, clear, hydrate, or change sync state for the current session, including same-phone reauthentication
mobile_client_contract.request_lifecycle_rule: bound token acquisition and each authenticated fetch pipeline to 15000 milliseconds; timeout retains byte-equivalent events and advances retry state, while caller cancellation or originating-session replacement cancels immediately without acknowledging, removing, rewriting, or incrementing retry state for queued events
mobile_client_contract.legacy_queue_rule: discard persisted generic sync_learning_state mutations during hydration, remove the pre-binding learning-event-outbox.v1 key without replaying its unbound entries, and never route active mobile learning completion through /v1/learning/state-sync

migration_boundary.legacy_v1_role: retained daily and learning snapshots are read-only migration input and are never accepted through HTTP after cutover; explicit daily check-in uses /v2/progress/check-in and is not a snapshot
migration_boundary.migrated_account_write_rule: the first accepted v2 event transaction preserves valid retained sequence-zero learning baselines for both tracks before closing migration; both v1 snapshot-write routes always return 410, while v2 check-in can only merge monotonic checked_in_today and cannot overwrite server-derived learning counts or canonical physical-space counts
migration_boundary.production_rule: production continues to reject all v1 routes
migration_boundary.launch_claim_rule: green repository-local backend, explicit check-in, scheduler, and mobile binding tests do not satisfy formal content approval, production deployment, or launch readiness

endpoint.identity_rule: derive the account only from the active v2 session; reject every query field except track and reject every request body

algorithm_contract.server_time_rule: apply newly accepted events to scheduler state at canonical server acceptance time; client_occurred_at remains bounded activity-day input and never overrides server_sequence ordering
algorithm_contract.visible_assessment_rule: the server-only mapping never expands the visible two-state self-assessment into four choices

projection_contract.atomicity: current selection validation, one new immutable event, account server sequence, learning projection, FSRS scheduler projection, learning-session projection watermark update and exact selection clearing, and daily progress commit in the same transaction
projection_contract.integrity: every positive-sequence latest card projection has exactly one matching validated scheduler state and no orphan scheduler state is accepted
projection_contract.duplicate_rule: exact event replay does not advance FSRS state, due time, cursor, sequence, or counters
projection_contract.legacy_rule: sequence-zero migrated cards have no invented FSRS history and remain immediately review-eligible until their first accepted v2 event
projection_contract.cursor_rule: a selected card persists in the independent account-and-track softbook_learning_sessions record as an opaque revisioned server cursor with an acknowledged-at plus latest-positive-server-sequence projection watermark; every newly accepted event for the track updates the timestamp component and advances the sequence component, first-event all-track migration synchronizes every migrated track while preserving valid sibling cursors, only one unseen completion carrying the exact current selection_id, card_id, phase, and content_version clears the cursor atomically, exact duplicate replay remains valid after clearing, and a session read requires the complete matching watermark plus transactional revision confirmation before returning a resumed cursor

selection_contract.single_card_rule: return at most one selection and never return card body content
selection_contract.existing_cursor_rule: resume an eligible persisted cursor before selecting another card only after its learning-projection watermark matches and its revision is transactionally confirmed
selection_contract.due_rule: select an accessible non-sleeping due review before every new card; order by earliest due time, then canonical card-source index, then card_id
selection_contract.new_card_rule: when no review is due, select the first accessible non-sleeping unseen card in normalized ordered card_records
selection_contract.future_rule: when no due review or new card exists, return selection null plus the earliest future next_due_at when one exists only after transactionally confirming the matching learning-projection watermark and session revision
selection_contract.sleep_rule: canonical physical-space sleeping state removes a card from resume, due, new, and next-due selection without deleting its learning or FSRS state
selection_contract.membership_rule: the first authenticated learning-session entry starts an available trial exactly once only after canonical context validation, selection generation, and required cursor persistence succeed; trial and premium may schedule the full library, while free schedules a stable release-scoped prefix of ceil(card_count * 0.5) in canonical card-source order
selection_contract.content_rule: selection binds to the exact normalized content_version and source; production still requires a matching published content-release.v1 descriptor

mobile_binding_contract.session_read_rule: remote mobile learning reads authenticated learning-session.v1 and the canonical card source, requires matching track, source_id, and content_version, and resolves only the returned card_id; it never applies client membership, sleep, review, or catalog ordering to replace the server selection
mobile_binding_contract.membership_reconciliation_rule: when learning-session.v1 membership_stage differs from the bootstrap snapshot, remote mobile refreshes canonical bootstrap and requires the exact stage before presenting the selection; it never synthesizes entitlement counters or dates from the session response
mobile_binding_contract.completion_rule: the mobile completion event copies the returned selection_id and phase and must match the selected card and exact content version before durable enqueue
mobile_binding_contract.advance_rule: after durable enqueue the completed card may leave the interaction surface, but no next card is selected until strict event acknowledgement, bootstrap reconciliation, and a fresh learning-session.v1 read
mobile_binding_contract.offline_rule: a previously validated cached selection and matching content may be answered once offline; without that pair learning stays blocked, and no second card may be selected locally
mobile_binding_contract.empty_rule: selection null is a valid server result and does not trigger bundled-card fallback or client ordering

server_scheduler_v1.launch_claim_rule: a green repository-local scheduler and mobile binding do not prove deployed integration, production entitlement, formal content approval, or launch readiness
